title: Microstructure and deformation behaviour of austenitic low-density steels: The defining role of B2 intermetallic phase
abstract_summary: This study investigates three hot-rolled austenitic low-density steels (Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C, Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C, Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C) to clarify the role of B2 ordered Fe(Ni)Al phase. The Ni-free alloy contains γ-matrix with fine κ-carbides, while Ni-containing grades exhibit coarser κ-carbides and B2 intermetallic. B2 presence increases strength but reduces tensile ductility; nanoindentation and orientation relationship analyses show co-deformation near yield and slip transfer control cracking and strain accumulation, with strain hardening differences attributed to dislocation substructure evolution.
research_objective: To elucidate the role of B2 ordered Fe(Ni)Al intermetallic phase on the microstructure and deformation behaviour of austenitic low-density steels.
material_systems: Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni-containing high-Mn LDS, γ-matrix + κ-carbides + B2)
Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni-containing low-Mn LDS, γ-matrix + coarser κ-carbides + B2)
Key phases: γ-austenite, κ-carbide (L12), B2 intermetallic (Fe(Ni)Al)
main_process_variables: Automated crystal orientation mapping in electron microscopy

sample_aliases.description: Austenitic low-density steel without nickel addition
Nickel-containing LDS with high manganese content
Nickel-containing LDS with low manganese content, containing bamboo-like stringer and polygonal B2

expected_information_axis: microstructure (phase constitution, B2 morphology, κ-carbide size, orientation relationships)
property (tensile strength, ductility, strain hardening behaviour, nanoindentation hardness, pop-in events)
mechanism (co-deformation, slip transfer, strain accumulation, B2 cracking, dislocation substructure evolution)
figure_evidence (micrographs, nanoindentation curves, TEM orientation maps)
key_sections: 1. Introduction (background on low-density steels, role of B2 phase, research motivation)
2. Experimental procedures (alloy preparation, hot rolling, characterization methods including tensile testing, nanoindentation, electron microscopy)
3. Results and discussion (microstructure analysis, nanoindentation responses, tensile properties, strain hardening behaviour, dislocation substructure evolution)
4. Conclusions (summary of B2 influence on strength, ductility and deformation mechanisms)
